title: Avoid exposing concrete XmlNode return types
messageFormat: Return an abstraction instead of a concrete XmlNode-derived type.
summary: Returning a concrete XmlNode, XmlElement, or XmlDocument ties every caller to the legacy DOM implementation and to the mutable object graph behind it. The API can no longer switch to a different XML representation without breaking consumers.
Expose a reader, a projected model, or an interface that conveys only what the caller needs.
